html:
𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄

THE ANCIENT COVENANT RECORD

Volume Five — Part Two

The Torah — Fifth Book

DEVARIM

Words

Chapters 18 through 34

The Prophet — The Cities of Refuge — The Covenant Curses — The Song of Moshe — His Death

PRIMARY MANUSCRIPT AUTHORITIES

1QDeuta (1Q4) · 1QDeutb (1Q5) · 4QDeuta through 4QDeutq

4QDeutn (4Q41) — The All-Souls Deuteronomy — Large format manuscript

11QDeut (11Q3) — Cave 11 Deuteronomy scroll

Ethiopian Orit Ge'ez — Beta Israel Ancient Canon

COMPARATIVE NOTES AT END OF EVERY CHAPTER

DSS · ORIT GE'EZ · MASORETIC VARIANT

YHWH / 𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄 and The Creator only — no substitutions

All personal names in original Hebrew form throughout

THE LAWS CONTINUED — CHAPTERS 18 THROUGH 26

DEVARIM — CHAPTER 18 The Levitical Priests — The True Prophet — The Test of Prophecy

18:1 The Levitical priests, all the tribe of Levi, shall have no portion or inheritance with Yisra'EL. They shall eat YHWH's food offerings as their inheritance.

18:2 They shall have no inheritance among their brothers — YHWH is their inheritance, as he promised them.

18:9 When you come into the land that YHWH your Creator is giving you, you shall not learn to follow the abominable practices of those nations.

18:10 There shall not be found among you anyone who burns his son or his daughter as an offering, anyone who practices divination or tells fortunes or interprets omens, or a sorcerer

18:11 Or a charmer or a medium or a necromancer or one who inquires of the dead,

18:12 For whoever does these things is an abomination to YHWH. And because of these abominations YHWH your Creator is driving them out before you.

18:14 For these nations, which you are about to dispossess, listen to fortune-tellers and to diviners. But as for you, YHWH your Creator has not allowed you to do this.

18:15 YHWH your Creator will raise up for you a prophet like me from among you, from your brothers — it is to him you shall listen.

18:18 I will raise up for them a prophet like you from among their brothers. And I will put my words in his mouth, and he shall speak to them all that I command him.

18:19 And whoever will not listen to my words that he shall speak in my name, I myself will require it of him.

18:20 But the prophet who presumes to speak a word in my name that I have not commanded him to speak, or who speaks in the name of other deities, that same prophet shall die.

18:21 And if you say in your heart: How may we know the word that YHWH has not spoken?

18:22 When a prophet speaks in the name of YHWH, if the word does not come to pass or come true, that is a word that YHWH has not spoken. The prophet has spoken it presumptuously. You need not be afraid of him.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QDeutm and 4QDeutn preserve portions of Chapter 18. The prophet-like-Moshe passage — verses 18:15-18 — is cited in 4QTestimonia (4Q175), the Testimonia scroll from Qumran, which collects key eschatological proof-texts from the Torah. The DSS community understood this passage as pointing to a future prophetic figure.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 18 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 18:15 — YHWH your Creator will raise up for you a prophet like me from among you, from your brothers — it is to him you shall listen. The promise of a future prophet like Moshe is one of the most significant prophetic passages in the Torah. The Testimonia scroll at Qumran lists it alongside Bila'am's Star oracle and the Levitical blessing as a key eschatological text. Devarim 34:10 states that no prophet like Moshe arose afterward in Yisra'EL — confirming the future prophet of Chapter 18 had not yet appeared at the close of the Torah. The ancient record holds the promise open.

DEVARIM — CHAPTER 19 Cities of Refuge — Witnesses — The Lex Talionis

19:1 When YHWH your Creator cuts off the nations whose land YHWH your Creator is giving you, and you dispossess them and dwell in their cities and in their houses,

19:2 You shall set apart three cities for yourselves in the land that YHWH your Creator is giving you to possess.

19:4 This is the provision for the manslayer, who by fleeing there may save his life. If anyone kills his neighbor unintentionally without having hated him in the past —

19:6 Lest the avenger of blood in hot anger pursue the manslayer and overtake him, because the way is long, and strike him fatally, though the man did not deserve to die, since he had not hated his neighbor in the past.

19:11 But if anyone hates his neighbor and lies in wait for him and attacks him and strikes him fatally so that he dies, and he flees into one of these cities,

19:12 Then the elders of his city shall send and take him from there, and hand him over to the avenger of blood, so that he may die.

19:15 A single witness shall not suffice against a person for any crime or for any wrong in connection with any offense that he has committed. Only on the evidence of two witnesses or of three witnesses shall a charge be established.

19:16 If a malicious witness arises to accuse a person of wrongdoing,

19:19 Then you shall do to him as he had meant to do to his brother. So you shall purge the evil from your midst.

19:21 Your eye shall not pity. It shall be life for life, eye for eye, tooth for tooth, hand for hand, foot for foot.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QDeutn and 4QDeuto preserve portions of Chapter 19.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 19 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 19:15 — a single witness shall not suffice for any crime or wrong. Only on the evidence of two or three witnesses shall a charge be established. The two-witness requirement is one of the most fundamental procedural protections in the Torah's legal system. No capital charge, no conviction on any offense, can proceed on one testimony alone. This principle — repeated in Bamidbar 35:30 and Devarim 17:6 — is the Torah's primary protection against false accusation. The legal system of the covenant community is designed with structural safeguards against the power of a single accuser.

DEVARIM — CHAPTER 20 The Laws of War — Who Is Exempt — The Treatment of Cities

20:1 When you go out to war against your enemies, and see horses and chariots and an army larger than your own, you shall not be afraid of them, for YHWH your Creator is with you, who brought you up out of the land of Egypt.

20:3 And shall say to them: Hear, O Yisra'EL, today you are drawing near for battle against your enemies. Let not your heart faint. Do not fear or panic or be in dread of them,

20:4 For YHWH your Creator is he who goes with you to fight for you against your enemies, to give you the victory.

20:5 Then the officers shall speak to the people, saying: Is there any man who has built a new house and has not dedicated it? Let him go back to his house, lest he die in the battle and another man dedicate it.

20:7 And is there any man who has betrothed a wife and has not taken her? Let him go back to his house, lest he die in the battle and another man take her.

20:8 And the officers shall speak further to the people, and say: Is there any man who is fearful and fainthearted? Let him go back to his house, lest he make the heart of his fellows melt like his own.

20:10 When you draw near to a city to fight against it, offer terms of peace to it.

20:19 When you besiege a city for a long time, making war against it in order to take it, you shall not destroy its trees by wielding an axe against them. You may eat from them, but you shall not cut them down. Are the trees in the field human, that they should be besieged by you?

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QDeuto and 4QDeutp preserve portions of Chapter 20.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 20 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 20:19 — you shall not destroy the trees of a city under siege. Are the trees in the field human, that they should be besieged by you? The covenant war law prohibits the destruction of food-producing trees even during active military siege. The question the Torah asks — are the trees human? — frames the prohibition in terms of the non-combatant status of the natural creation. Trees do not choose sides. They produce food. Destroying them punishes the land and future generations for a conflict that is human. The ancient covenant law of war includes an early form of environmental protection built into military ethics.

DEVARIM — CHAPTER 21 The Unsolved Murder — The Captive Woman — The Firstborn Rights — The Rebellious Son

21:1 If in the land that YHWH your Creator is giving you to possess, anyone is found slain, lying in the open country, and it is not known who killed him,

21:3 And the elders of the city that is nearest to the slain man shall take a heifer that has never been worked and that has not pulled in a yoke.

21:4 And the elders of that city shall bring the heifer down to a valley with running water, which is neither plowed nor sown, and shall break the heifer's neck there in the valley.

21:6 And all the elders of that city nearest to the slain man shall wash their hands over the heifer whose neck was broken in the valley,

21:7 And they shall testify: Our hands did not shed this blood, nor did our eyes see it shed.

21:8 Accept atonement, O YHWH, for your people Yisra'EL, whom you have redeemed, and do not set the guilt of innocent blood in the midst of your people Yisra'EL, so that their blood guilt be atoned for.

21:15 If a man has two wives, the one loved and the other unloved, and both the loved and the unloved have borne him children, and if the firstborn son belongs to the unloved,

21:17 But he shall acknowledge the firstborn, the son of the unloved, by giving him a double portion of all that he has, for he is the firstfruits of his strength. The right of the firstborn is his.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QDeutp and 4QDeutq preserve portions of Chapter 21.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 21 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verses 21:1-8 — the ritual of the broken-necked heifer for an unsolved murder. When an unidentified body is found, the elders of the nearest city perform an atonement ritual, wash their hands, and declare their community's innocence. The covenant law provides a mechanism for dealing with the moral pollution of shed innocent blood even when the murderer is unknown. The land must not carry unresolved bloodguilt — Bamidbar 35:33. The ritual transfers the community's declaration of innocence and makes atonement for the unresolved case. The covenant takes seriously even deaths that cannot be prosecuted.

DEVARIM — CHAPTER 22 Various Laws — The Woman's Garment — The Parapets — Sexual Laws

22:1 You shall not see your brother's ox or his sheep going astray and ignore them. You shall take them back to your brother.

22:4 You shall not see your brother's donkey or his ox fallen down by the way and ignore them. You shall help him to lift them up again.

22:6 If you come across a bird's nest in any tree or on the ground, with young ones or eggs and the mother sitting on the young or on the eggs, you shall not take the mother with the young.

22:7 You shall let the mother go, but the young you may take for yourself, that it may go well with you, and that you may live long.

22:8 When you build a new house, you shall make a parapet for your roof, that you may not bring the guilt of blood upon your house, if anyone should fall from it.

22:10 You shall not plow with an ox and a donkey together.

22:11 You shall not wear cloth of wool and linen mixed together.

22:12 You shall make yourself tassels on the four corners of the garment with which you cover yourself.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QDeutq and 1QDeuta preserve portions of Chapter 22.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 22 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 22:6-7 — you shall not take the mother bird with the young — let the mother go. The mother bird law — shiluakh haken — is one of the statutes given with an explicit reward: that it may go well with you and that you may live long. The same reward formula appears in the commandment to honor father and mother — Shemot 20:12. The ancient covenant law extends ethical consideration to non-human creatures. A mother bird's relationship to her eggs and young is given legal standing. Gratuitous destruction of that relationship is prohibited even when the young are being taken for food.

DEVARIM — CHAPTER 23 Those Excluded from the Assembly — Various Social Laws

23:1 No one whose testicles are crushed or whose male organ is cut off shall enter the assembly of YHWH.

23:3 No Ammonite or Mo'avite may enter the assembly of YHWH. Even to the tenth generation, none of them may enter the assembly of YHWH forever,

23:4 Because they did not meet you with bread and with water on the way, when you came out of Egypt, and because they hired against you Bila'am the son of Be'or from Petor of Mesopotamia, to curse you.

23:7 You shall not abhor an Edomite, for he is your brother. You shall not abhor an Egyptian, because you were a sojourner in his land.

23:8 Children born to them in the third generation may enter the assembly of YHWH.

23:15 You shall not give up to his master a slave who has escaped from his master to you.

23:19 You shall not charge interest on loans to your brother, interest on money, interest on food, interest on anything that is lent for interest.

23:21 If you make a vow to YHWH your Creator, you shall not delay fulfilling it, for YHWH your Creator will surely require it of you, and you will be guilty of sin.

23:23 You shall be careful to do what has passed your lips, for you have voluntarily vowed to YHWH your Creator what you have promised with your mouth.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QDeutq and 1QDeuta preserve portions of Chapter 23.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 23 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 23:15 — you shall not give up to his master a slave who has escaped from his master to you. The covenant law of Devarim provides asylum to escaped slaves. An escaped slave who reaches Yisra'EL may not be returned. He shall dwell with you in your midst wherever he chooses. This is one of the most striking covenant social laws in the Torah — it directly contradicts the common ancient Near Eastern practice of slave return treaties. The covenant community is designated as a place of refuge for the escapee. The Exodus memory grounds this: YHWH delivered slaves from Egypt. The covenant people do not re-enslave those who have escaped.

DEVARIM — CHAPTER 24 Divorce — Pledges — Wages — Gleaning — The Poor

24:1 When a man takes a wife and marries her, if then she finds no favor in his eyes because he has found some indecency in her, and he writes her a certificate of divorce and puts it in her hand and sends her out of his house, and she departs out of his house,

24:5 When a man is newly married, he shall not go out with the army or be liable for any other public duty. He shall be free at home one year to be happy with his wife whom he has taken.

24:10 When you make your neighbor a loan of any sort, you shall not go into his house to collect his pledge.

24:12 And if he is a poor man, you shall not sleep in his pledge.

24:13 You shall restore to him the pledge as the sun sets, that he may sleep in his cloak and bless you. And it shall be righteousness for you before YHWH your Creator.

24:14 You shall not oppress a hired worker who is poor and needy, whether he is one of your brothers or one of the sojourners who are in your land within your towns.

24:15 You shall give him his wages on the same day, before the sun sets — for he is poor and counts on it — lest he cry against you to YHWH, and you be guilty of sin.

24:16 Fathers shall not be put to death because of their children, nor shall children be put to death because of their fathers. Each one shall be put to death for his own sin.

24:17 You shall not pervert the justice due to the sojourner or to the fatherless, or take a widow's garment in pledge,

24:18 But you shall remember that you were a slave in Egypt and YHWH your Creator redeemed you from there — therefore I command you to do this.

24:19 When you reap your harvest in your field and forget a sheaf in the field, you shall not go back to get it. It shall be for the sojourner, the fatherless, and the widow, that YHWH your Creator may bless you in all the work of your hands.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 1QDeuta and 4QDeuta preserve portions of Chapter 24.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 24 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 24:16 — fathers shall not be put to death because of their children, nor children because of their fathers — each one shall be put to death for his own sin. The principle of individual moral accountability is stated here with absolute clarity. Covenant justice does not hold families collectively liable for the crimes of individual members. Each person is accountable for his own sin. This principle is reaffirmed and expanded in Yehezkel 18, where the prophet argues the same point against the proverb of the fathers eating sour grapes and the children's teeth being set on edge. Individual accountability is a foundational principle of ancient Hebrew covenant law.

DEVARIM — CHAPTER 25 Flogging — The Levirate Marriage — Honest Weights — Amalek

25:1 If there is a dispute between men and they come into court and the judges decide between them, acquitting the innocent and condemning the guilty,

25:2 Then if the guilty man deserves to be beaten, the judge shall cause him to lie down and be beaten in his presence with a number of stripes in proportion to his offense.

25:3 Forty stripes may be given him, but not more, lest, if one should go on to beat him with more stripes than these, your brother be degraded in your sight.

25:4 You shall not muzzle an ox when it is treading out the grain.

25:5 If brothers dwell together, and one of them dies and has no son, the wife of the dead man shall not be married outside the family to a stranger. Her husband's brother shall go in to her and take her as his wife and perform the duty of a husband's brother to her.

25:13 You shall not have in your bag two kinds of weights, a large and a small.

25:15 A full and fair weight you shall have, a full and fair measure you shall have, that your days may be long in the land that YHWH your Creator is giving you.

25:16 For all who do such things, all who act dishonestly, are an abomination to YHWH your Creator.

25:17 Remember what Amalek did to you on the way as you came out of Egypt,

25:19 Therefore when YHWH your Creator has given you rest from all your enemies around you, in the land that YHWH your Creator is giving you for an inheritance to possess, you shall blot out the memory of Amalek. You shall not forget.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QDeuta and 4QDeutb preserve portions of Chapter 25.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 25 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 25:3 — forty stripes but not more, lest your brother be degraded in your sight. Even judicial corporal punishment has a ceiling in the Torah. The punishment limit is framed in terms of dignity: the guilty man remains your brother even when being punished. Exceeding forty stripes degrades him in your sight. The covenant law insists on the retained human dignity of the convicted. Later Jewish legal practice interpreted this as thirty-nine stripes to ensure the forty limit was never exceeded — a margin of protection around the commanded limit.

DEVARIM — CHAPTER 26 The Firstfruits Confession — The Third-Year Tithe Declaration — The Covenant Conclusion

26:1 When you come into the land that YHWH your Creator is giving you for an inheritance and have taken possession of it and live in it,

26:2 You shall take some of the first of all the fruit of the ground, which you harvest from your land that YHWH your Creator is giving you, and you shall put it in a basket, and you shall go to the place that YHWH your Creator will choose, to make his name to dwell there.

26:5 And you shall make response before YHWH your Creator: A wandering Aramean was my father. And he went down into Egypt and sojourned there, few in number, and there he became a nation, great, mighty, and populous.

26:6 And the Egyptians treated us harshly and humiliated us and laid on us hard labor.

26:7 Then we cried to YHWH, The Creator of our fathers, and YHWH heard our voice and saw our affliction, our toil, and our oppression.

26:8 And YHWH brought us out of Egypt with a mighty hand and an outstretched arm, with great deeds of terror, with signs and wonders.

26:9 And he brought us into this place and gave us this land, a land flowing with milk and honey.

26:10 And behold, now I bring the first of the fruit of the ground, which you, O YHWH, have given me. And you shall set it down before YHWH your Creator and worship before YHWH your Creator.

26:17 You have declared today that YHWH is your Creator, and that you will walk in his ways, and keep his statutes and his commandments and his rules, and will obey his voice.

26:18 And YHWH has declared today that you are a people for his treasured possession, as he has promised you, and that you are to keep all his commandments.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QDeutb and 4QDeutc preserve portions of Chapter 26.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 26 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 26:5-9 — a wandering Aramean was my father. The firstfruits confession is the earliest Hebrew historical creed in the Torah. It compresses the entire covenant narrative into seven verses: the patriarchal origins, the descent to Egypt, the oppression, the cry, YHWH's hearing, the Exodus with signs and wonders, and the entry into the land. This confession is spoken by each individual Yisra'ELi when bringing firstfruits to the sanctuary. The national history is rehearsed in first-person singular: we cried, YHWH heard, he brought us out, he gave us this land. The covenant history is personal. Each covenant member owns it as their own story.

THE COVENANT CEREMONY — THE BLESSINGS AND CURSES — CHAPTERS 27 THROUGH 30

DEVARIM — CHAPTER 27 The Covenant Ceremony at Shechem — The Twelve Curses

27:1 Now Moshe and the elders of Yisra'EL commanded the people, saying: Keep the whole commandment that I command you today.

27:2 And on the day you cross over the Yarden to the land that YHWH your Creator is giving you, you shall set up large stones and plaster them with plaster.

27:3 And you shall write on them all the words of this law, when you cross over to enter the land that YHWH your Creator is giving you, a land flowing with milk and honey, as YHWH, The Creator of your fathers, promised you.

27:5 And there you shall build an altar to YHWH your Creator, an altar of stones. You shall wield no iron tool on them.

27:11 And Moshe commanded the people the same day, saying:

27:12 When you have crossed over the Yarden, these shall stand on Mount Gerizim to bless the people: Shim'on, Levi, Yehudah, Yissakhar, Yosef, and Binyamin.

27:13 And these shall stand on Mount Eval for the curse: Re'uven, Gad, Asher, Zevulun, Dan, and Naftali.

27:15 Cursed be the man who makes a carved or cast metal image, an abomination to YHWH, a thing made by the hands of a craftsman, and sets it up in secret. And all the people shall answer and say: Amen.

27:19 Cursed be anyone who perverts the justice due to the sojourner, the fatherless, and the widow. And all the people shall say: Amen.

27:26 Cursed be anyone who does not confirm the words of this law by doing them. And all the people shall say: Amen.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QDeutc and 4QDeutd preserve portions of Chapter 27.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 27 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] The covenant ceremony at Shechem — the valley between Mount Gerizim and Mount Eval — is the ratification of the covenant on the land itself. The Torah written on plastered stones, an altar built of uncut stones, blessings pronounced from one mountain and curses from the other, the entire people responding Amen to each curse. The covenant is sealed in the geography of the land. The twelve curses of Chapter 27 cover violations done in secret — hidden idolatry, dishonoring parents, boundary moving, misleading the blind, perverting justice for the vulnerable. The public covenant ceremony makes the hidden violations public before YHWH. The Amen is a collective acknowledgment that these curses apply.

DEVARIM — CHAPTER 28 The Covenant Blessings — The Covenant Curses — The Full Warning

28:1 And if you faithfully obey the voice of YHWH your Creator, being careful to do all his commandments that I command you today, YHWH your Creator will set you high above all the nations of the earth.

28:2 And all these blessings shall come upon you and overtake you, if you obey the voice of YHWH your Creator.

28:3 Blessed shall you be in the city, and blessed shall you be in the field.

28:4 Blessed shall be the fruit of your womb and the fruit of your ground and the fruit of your cattle, the increase of your herds and the young of your flock.

28:9 YHWH will establish you as a people holy to himself, as he has sworn to you, if you keep the commandments of YHWH your Creator and walk in his ways.

28:10 And all the peoples of the earth shall see that you are called by the name of YHWH, and they shall be afraid of you.

28:15 But if you will not obey the voice of YHWH your Creator or be careful to do all his commandments and his statutes that I command you today, then all these curses shall come upon you and overtake you.

28:25 YHWH will cause you to be defeated before your enemies. You shall go out one way against them and flee seven ways before them. And you shall be a horror to all the kingdoms of the earth.

28:36 YHWH will bring you and your king whom you set over you to a nation that neither you nor your fathers have known. And there you shall serve other deities, of wood and stone.

28:47 Because you did not serve YHWH your Creator with joyfulness and gladness of heart, because of the abundance of all things,

28:48 Therefore you shall serve your enemies whom YHWH will send against you, in hunger and thirst, in nakedness, and lacking everything. And he will put a yoke of iron on your neck until he has destroyed you.

28:63 And as YHWH took delight in doing you good and multiplying you, so YHWH will take delight in bringing ruin upon you and destroying you. And you shall be plucked off the land that you are entering to take possession of it.

28:64 And YHWH will scatter you among all peoples, from one end of the earth to the other, and there you shall serve other deities of wood and stone, which neither you nor your fathers have known.

28:67 In the morning you shall say: If only it were evening — and at evening you shall say: If only it were morning — because of the dread that your heart shall feel, and the sights that your eyes shall see.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QDeutd and 4QDeute preserve portions of Chapter 28. Chapter 28 is the longest chapter in Devarim and one of the most attested in the Cave 4 manuscripts.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 28 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verses 28:47-48 — because you did not serve YHWH your Creator with joyfulness and gladness of heart, because of the abundance of all things, therefore you shall serve your enemies in hunger and thirst. The curses of Chapter 28 include a striking diagnosis: the failure was not primarily in overt rebellion but in joyless covenant service. The abundance of blessing became the condition in which gratitude and joy ceased. Serving YHWH without gladness in abundance is the seed of the covenant breach. The contrast between joyful service of YHWH and forced service of enemies is the ancient record's most pointed statement about the inner disposition the covenant requires.

DEVARIM — CHAPTER 29 The Covenant Renewed in Mo'av — The Warning of Complacency

29:1 These are the words of the covenant that YHWH commanded Moshe to make with the people of Yisra'EL in the land of Mo'av, besides the covenant that he had made with them at Horev.

29:2 And Moshe summoned all Yisra'EL and said to them: You have seen all that YHWH did before your eyes in the land of Egypt, to Pharaoh and to all his servants and to all his land,

29:4 But to this day YHWH has not given you a heart to understand or eyes to see or ears to hear.

29:9 Therefore keep the words of this covenant and do them, that you may prosper in all that you do.

29:10 You are standing today, all of you, before YHWH your Creator: the heads of your tribes, your elders, and your officers, all the men of Yisra'EL,

29:11 Your little ones, your wives, and the sojourner who is in your camp, from the one who chops your wood to the one who draws your water,

29:12 So that you may enter into the sworn covenant of YHWH your Creator, which YHWH your Creator is making with you today,

29:14 I am making this sworn covenant not with you alone,

29:15 But with whoever is standing here with us today before YHWH our Creator, and with whoever is not here with us today.

29:29 The secret things belong to YHWH our Creator, but the things that are revealed belong to us and to our children forever, that we may do all the words of this law.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QDeute and 4QDeutf preserve portions of Chapter 29.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 29 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 29:15 — I am making this sworn covenant not with you alone, but with whoever is not here with us today. The Mo'av covenant renewal explicitly extends to every future generation of the covenant people who are not physically present at the renewal ceremony. The covenant is not limited to those standing on that plain in Yisra'EL's fortieth year. Every subsequent generation of Yisra'EL enters the covenant through this extension. The covenant community across time is united by this one declaration: future generations are included in the sworn covenant made in Mo'av.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 29:29 — the secret things belong to YHWH our Creator, but the things that are revealed belong to us and to our children forever, that we may do all the words of this law. The Torah draws a deliberate boundary between divine hiddenness and divine disclosure. YHWH has secrets that belong to him alone. What he has disclosed — the covenant law — belongs to the covenant people and their children. The obligation is calibrated to the disclosure. The covenant people are not responsible for what YHWH has not revealed. They are fully responsible for what he has revealed.

DEVARIM — CHAPTER 30 The Restoration Promise — Return and Circumcision of the Heart

30:1 And when all these things come upon you, the blessing and the curse, which I have set before you, and you call them to mind among all the nations where YHWH your Creator has driven you,

30:2 And return to YHWH your Creator, you and your children, and obey his voice in all that I command you today, with all your heart and with all your soul,

30:3 Then YHWH your Creator will restore your fortunes and have mercy on you, and he will gather you again from all the peoples where YHWH your Creator has scattered you.

30:4 If your outcasts are in the uttermost parts of heaven, from there YHWH your Creator will gather you, and from there he will take you.

30:6 And YHWH your Creator will circumcise your heart and the heart of your offspring, so that you will love YHWH your Creator with all your heart and with all your soul, that you may live.

30:9 For YHWH will again take delight in prospering you, as he took delight in your fathers,

30:10 When you obey the voice of YHWH your Creator, to keep his commandments and his statutes that are written in this Book of the Law, when you turn to YHWH your Creator with all your heart and with all your soul.

30:11 For this commandment that I command you today is not too hard for you, neither is it far off.

30:12 It is not in heaven, that you should say: Who will ascend to heaven for us and bring it to us, that we may hear it and do it?

30:14 But the word is very near you. It is in your mouth and in your heart, so that you can do it.

30:19 I call heaven and earth to witness against you today, that I have set before you life and death, blessing and curse. Therefore choose life, that you and your offspring may live,

30:20 Loving YHWH your Creator, obeying his voice and holding fast to him, for he is your life and length of days, that you may dwell in the land that YHWH swore to your fathers, to Avraham, to Yitzhak, and to Yaakov, to give them.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QDeutf and 4QDeutg preserve portions of Chapter 30. The restoration promise and the circumcision of the heart passage are attested in multiple Cave 4 manuscripts.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 30 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 30:6 — YHWH your Creator will circumcise your heart and the heart of your offspring, so that you will love YHWH your Creator with all your heart and with all your soul, that you may live. This is the Torah's own promise of inner covenant transformation. Physical circumcision — the covenant sign of Bereshit 17 — has an inner counterpart that YHWH himself will perform in the restoration. The circumcised heart loves YHWH with all its heart and soul — fulfilling the Shema's requirement. Moshe calls for circumcision of the heart in Devarim 10:16. Here in Chapter 30 YHWH promises to do it himself. The covenant's completion is YHWH's own act on the interior of the covenant people.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 30:19-20 — I call heaven and earth to witness: I have set before you life and death, blessing and curse. Therefore choose life. The covenant's ultimate call is a choice. YHWH does not coerce. He sets two paths — life and death, blessing and curse — and commands the choice of life. Loving YHWH, obeying his voice, holding fast to him — these are the content of choosing life. The covenant is not self-executing. It requires the ongoing willing participation of the covenant people. Heaven and earth are called as witnesses to the offer made.

THE FINAL DAYS OF MOSHE — CHAPTERS 31 THROUGH 34

DEVARIM — CHAPTER 31 The Commissioning of Yehoshua — The Torah Written — The Song Commanded

31:1 So Moshe continued to speak these words to all Yisra'EL.

31:2 And he said to them: I am 120 years old today. I am no longer able to go out and come in. YHWH has said to me: You shall not go over this Yarden.

31:3 YHWH your Creator himself will go over before you. He will destroy these nations before you, so that you shall dispossess them, and Yehoshua will go over before you, as YHWH has spoken.

31:6 Be strong and courageous. Do not fear or be in dread of them, for it is YHWH your Creator who goes with you. He will not leave you or forsake you.

31:8 It is YHWH who goes before you. He will be with you. He will not leave you or forsake you. Do not fear or be dismayed.

31:9 Then Moshe wrote this law and gave it to the priests, the sons of Levi, who carried the ark of the covenant of YHWH, and to all the elders of Yisra'EL.

31:10 And Moshe commanded them: At the end of every seven years, at the set time in the year of release, at the Feast of Booths,

31:11 When all Yisra'EL comes to appear before YHWH your Creator at the place that he will choose, you shall read this law before all Yisra'EL in their hearing.

31:12 Assemble the people, men, women, and little ones, and the sojourner within your towns, that they may hear and learn to fear YHWH your Creator, and be careful to do all the words of this law,

31:13 And that their children, who have not known it, may hear and learn to fear YHWH your Creator, as long as you live in the land that you are going over the Yarden to possess.

31:19 Now therefore write this song and teach it to the people of Yisra'EL. Put it in their mouths, that this song may be a witness for me against the people of Yisra'EL.

31:23 And YHWH commissioned Yehoshua the son of Nun and said: Be strong and courageous, for you shall bring the people of Yisra'EL into the land that I swore to give them. I will be with you.

31:24 When Moshe had finished writing the words of this law in a book to the very end,

31:25 Moshe commanded the Levites who carried the ark of the covenant of YHWH:

31:26 Take this Book of the Law and put it by the side of the ark of the covenant of YHWH your Creator, that it may be there for a witness against you.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QDeutg and 4QDeuth preserve portions of Chapter 31. Verse 31:9 — Moshe wrote this law — and verse 31:24 — when Moshe had finished writing the words of this law in a book to the very end — are the Torah's own explicit statements of Moshe's authorship. Both are attested in the Cave 4 manuscripts.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 31 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 31:9 and 31:24-26 — Moshe wrote this law and placed it beside the ark. The Torah's own testimony to its written composition by Moshe is explicit and double: written and placed. The Book of the Law — sefer hatorah — is finished, physically written, and deposited beside the ark as a permanent covenant witness. The Torah does not merely claim to record Moshe's words. It claims to be Moshe's written document, placed in the most sacred location in the covenant community — beside the ark of the covenant of YHWH. This is the Torah's own account of its origin.

DEVARIM — CHAPTER 32 The Song of Moshe — Ha'azinu — The Complete Song

32:1 Give ear, O heavens, and I will speak, and let the earth hear the words of my mouth.

32:2 May my teaching drop as the rain, my speech distill as the dew, like gentle rain upon the tender grass, and like showers upon the herb.

32:3 For I will proclaim the name of YHWH — ascribe greatness to our Creator.

32:4 The Rock, his work is perfect, for all his ways are justice. A Creator of faithfulness and without iniquity, just and upright is he.

32:5 They have dealt corruptly with him — they are no longer his children because they are blemished — they are a crooked and twisted generation.

32:6 Do you thus repay YHWH, you foolish and senseless people? Is not he your father, who created you, who made you and established you?

32:7 Remember the days of old — consider the years of many generations. Ask your father, and he will show you, your elders, and they will tell you.

32:8 When the Most High gave to the nations their inheritance, when he divided mankind, he fixed the borders of the peoples according to the number of the sons of The Creator.

32:9 But YHWH's portion is his people — Yaakov his allotted heritage.

32:10 He found him in a desert land and in the howling waste of the wilderness — he encircled him, he cared for him, he kept him as the apple of his eye.

32:11 Like an eagle that stirs up its nest, that flutters over its young, spreading out its wings, catching them, bearing them on its pinions —

32:12 YHWH alone guided him, no foreign deity was with him.

32:15 But Yeshurun grew fat and kicked — you grew fat, stout, and sleek — then he forsook The Creator who made him and scoffed at the Rock of his salvation.

32:18 You were unmindful of the Rock that bore you, and you forgot The Creator who gave you birth.

32:20 And he said: I will hide my face from them — I will see what their end will be, for they are a perverse generation, children in whom is no faithfulness.

32:30 How could one have chased a thousand, and two have put ten thousand to flight, unless their Rock had sold them, and YHWH had given them up?

32:36 For YHWH will vindicate his people and have compassion on his servants, when he sees that their power is gone and there is none remaining, bond or free.

32:39 See now that I, even I, am he, and there is no deity beside me — I kill and I make alive — I wound and I heal — and there is none that can deliver out of my hand.

32:43 Rejoice with him, O heavens — bow down to him, all deities — for he avenges the blood of his children and takes vengeance on his adversaries. He repays those who hate him and cleanses his people's land.

32:46 He said to them: Take to heart all the words by which I am warning you today, that you may command them to your children, that they may be careful to do all the words of this law.

32:47 For it is no empty word for you, but your very life, and by this word you shall live long in the land that you are going over the Yarden to possess.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QDeuth, 4QDeuti, and multiple other Cave 4 manuscripts preserve portions of Chapter 32. The Song of Moshe is one of the most attested passages in the Qumran Devarim corpus. Verse 32:8 is a critical textual variant: the Masoretic text reads according to the number of the sons of Yisra'EL — bene Yisra'EL. The DSS — 4QDeutj — and the Septuagint read according to the number of the sons of The Creator — bene Elohim. The DSS reading connects this verse to the divine council cosmology of Tehillim 82 and the Chanokh literature. The DSS reading is preserved as the primary text.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 32 in full including the Song of Moshe.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] Significant variant at verse 32:8 — DSS and Septuagint read sons of The Creator where the Masoretic reads sons of Yisra'EL. The DSS reading is followed.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 32:8-9 — when the Most High divided mankind, he fixed the borders of the peoples according to the number of the sons of The Creator. But YHWH's portion is his people — Yaakov his allotted heritage. The DSS reading of verse 32:8 places the division of the nations at creation within a divine council framework: The Creator assigned each nation a territory corresponding to a member of the divine council — the sons of The Creator. YHWH's own portion in this cosmic distribution is Yaakov — the covenant people. The nations have their territories and their heavenly overseers. YHWH himself takes Yisra'EL. This cosmology is consistent with Tehillim 82 — YHWH judges the divine council — and with the Chanokh literature's extensive treatment of the Watchers assigned over the nations. The Masoretic reading — sons of Yisra'EL — is a later alteration that removes the divine council dimension.

DEVARIM — CHAPTER 33 The Blessing of Moshe — The Final Blessing on Each Tribe

33:1 This is the blessing with which Moshe the man of The Creator blessed the people of Yisra'EL before his death.

33:2 He said: YHWH came from Sinai and dawned from Se'ir upon them — he shone forth from Mount Paran — he came from the ten thousands of holy ones, with flaming fire at his right hand.

33:3 Yes, he loved his people, all his holy ones were in his hand — and they bowed down at his feet, everyone who received from his words.

33:5 Thus YHWH became king in Yeshurun, when the heads of the people were gathered, all the tribes of Yisra'EL together.

33:6 Let Re'uven live and not die, but let his men be few.

33:7 And this he said of Yehudah: Hear, O YHWH, the voice of Yehudah, and bring him in to his people. With your hands contend for him, and be a help against his adversaries.

33:8 And of Levi he said: Give to Levi your Thummim, and your Urim to your godly one, whom you tested at Massah, with whom you quarreled at the waters of Merivah.

33:12 Of Binyamin he said: The beloved of YHWH dwells in safety. The High One surrounds him all day long, and dwells between his shoulders.

33:13 And of Yosef he said: Blessed by YHWH be his land, with the choicest gifts of heaven above, and of the deep that crouches beneath.

33:20 And of Gad he said: Blessed be he who enlarges Gad. Gad crouches like a lion — he tears off arm and scalp.

33:22 And of Dan he said: Dan is a lion's cub that leaps from Bashan.

33:23 And of Naftali he said: O Naftali, sated with favor, and full of the blessing of YHWH — possess the lake and the south.

33:24 And of Asher he said: Most blessed of sons be Asher — let him be the favorite of his brothers, and let him dip his foot in oil.

33:26 There is none like The Creator, O Yeshurun, who rides through the heavens to your help, through the skies in his majesty.

33:27 The eternal Creator is your dwelling place, and underneath are the everlasting arms. And he thrust out the enemy before you and said: Destroy.

33:29 Happy are you, O Yisra'EL — who is like you, a people saved by YHWH, the shield of your help, and the sword of your triumph. Your enemies shall come fawning to you, and you shall tread upon their backs.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QDeuti and 4QDeutj preserve portions of Chapter 33. The blessing of Moshe is attested in multiple Cave 4 manuscripts.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 33 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 33:1 — the blessing with which Moshe the man of The Creator blessed the people of Yisra'EL before his death. The individual tribal blessings of Chapter 33 parallel Yaakov's blessings in Bereshit 49. Both are deathbed blessings from covenant leaders over the twelve tribes. Moshe blesses the people he has led for forty years. The tribes are addressed individually, each receiving a word that touches their character, territory, and destiny. Shim'on is absent from Moshe's blessings — the only tribe not mentioned. The ancient record offers no explanation. The silence is preserved in the DSS manuscripts as in the Masoretic.

DEVARIM — CHAPTER 34 The Death of Moshe — The End of the Torah

34:1 Then Moshe went up from the plains of Mo'av to Mount Nevo, to the top of Pisgah, which is opposite Yeriho. And YHWH showed him all the land, Gil'ad as far as Dan,

34:2 All Naftali, the land of Efrayim and Menasheh, all the land of Yehudah as far as the western sea,

34:3 The Negev, and the Plain, that is, the Valley of Yeriho the city of palm trees, as far as Tzoar.

34:4 And YHWH said to him: This is the land of which I swore to Avraham, to Yitzhak, and to Yaakov: I will give it to your offspring. I have let you see it with your eyes, but you shall not go over there.

34:5 So Moshe the servant of YHWH died there in the land of Mo'av, according to the word of YHWH.

34:6 And he buried him in the valley in the land of Mo'av opposite Beit-Pe'or, but no one knows the place of his burial to this day.

34:7 Moshe was 120 years old when he died. His eye was undimmed and his vigor unabated.

34:8 And the people of Yisra'EL wept for Moshe in the plains of Mo'av thirty days. Then the days of weeping and mourning for Moshe were ended.

34:9 And Yehoshua the son of Nun was full of the spirit of wisdom, for Moshe had laid his hands on him. So the people of Yisra'EL obeyed him and did as YHWH had commanded Moshe.

34:10 And there has not arisen a prophet since in Yisra'EL like Moshe, whom YHWH knew face to face,

34:11 None like him for all the signs and the wonders that YHWH sent him to do in the land of Egypt, to Pharaoh and to all his servants and to all his land,

34:12 And for all the mighty power and all the great deeds of terror that Moshe did in the sight of all Yisra'EL.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QDeutq and 1QDeutb preserve portions of Chapter 34. The death and burial of Moshe and the declaration of verse 34:10 — no prophet has arisen like Moshe — are attested in the Cave 4 and Cave 1 manuscripts. The Book of Yovelim (Jubilees 47-48) provides additional narrative material on Moshe's life and death from the Ethiopian canon and the Qumran community's expanded treatment. The Assumption of Moshe — Ascension of Moses — a text related to but distinct from the Qumran corpus, deals with Moshe's death and is referenced in the Epistle of Yehudah in the New Testament period literature, indicating it was known in the Second Temple period.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 34 in full. The Ethiopian Beta Israel tradition preserves additional accounts of Moshe's death connected to the Ge'ez manuscript tradition.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 34:6 — he buried him in the valley in the land of Mo'av, but no one knows the place of his burial to this day. YHWH buries Moshe. No human hands inter him. The greatest prophet in Yisra'EL's history has a grave that no one can locate — deliberately concealed by YHWH himself. The ancient record gives no explanation for this concealment. Several ancient traditions speculate: to prevent the grave from becoming a site of veneration, to deny enemies the satisfaction of knowing where he lies, to preserve the mystery of his departure. The Torah closes the account by naming the location — opposite Beit-Pe'or, the site of the Baal-Pe'or apostasy in Bamidbar 25 — and simultaneously stating that no one has ever found the grave.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 34:10-12 — no prophet has arisen in Yisra'EL like Moshe, whom YHWH knew face to face, for all the signs and wonders YHWH sent him to do in Egypt, and for all the mighty power and great deeds of terror that Moshe did in the sight of all Yisra'EL. The Torah closes with its own assessment of Moshe: incomparable, unmatched, and unrepeated. The face-to-face relationship with YHWH — stated at Shemot 33:11 and Bamidbar 12:8 — is the defining characteristic of his prophetic standing. The signs in Egypt and the mighty power before Yisra'EL are the outward confirmation of the inner standing. The promise of a prophet like Moshe in Devarim 18:15 stands open at the Torah's close. The greatest figure in the covenant's history has died. His successor is commissioned. The land awaits. The Torah ends not with arrival but with anticipation.

END OF DEVARIM — VOLUME FIVE COMPLETE

The Fifth Book of the Torah — Chapters 1 through 34
